observations.motif: Death and Burial of a Spouse
observations.summary: The passage describes Father Aviel burying his bride beneath the earth, symbolizing death and burial, a common biblical motif representing loss and mourning.

evidence.quote: when they buried his bride beneath the earth

observations.motif: Loss of Faith and Abandonment of Idols
observations.summary: Father Aviel sweeps away the gods he carried except one, reflecting the biblical motif of abandoning false gods and idols in times of crisis or loss.

evidence.quote: The gathered gods he'd carried from the Western plains—bronze and brittle forms of fortune, flame, and fertility—could not fill the void. So he swept them away and kept only one
